key_insight: Uncertainty-guided recovery demonstrations make VLA fine-tuning more efficient than passive imitation, but targeted updates alone can cause catastrophic forgetting.
engineering_relevance: For robotics and autonomy pipelines, active data collection can reduce wasted demonstration effort while improving adaptation to failure cases in vision-language-action policies.
method: Use active, uncertainty-guided recovery data collection instead of waiting for full task failure
Fine-tune autoregressive VLA policies with newly collected recovery demonstrations
Compare continual-learning strategies such as replay-based mixing and elastic weight consolidation
evidence_signal: The paper reports that active recovery data improves adaptation efficiency over passive demonstrations, and that continual-learning methods are needed to preserve prior behaviors during fine-tuning.
watch_item: Purely training on new recovery data risks catastrophic forgetting, so deployment needs a retention strategy such as replay or regularization.
paper_title: RECALL: Recovery Experience Collection for Active Lifelong Learning in Vision-Language-Action Models
